steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase "counterintuitive effect" signals a contrast. Normally, higher prices reduce demand, but here buyers started rushing to purchase figures because they believed prices would rise further—this means the missing word must express 'causing demand.'

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank describes the "counterintuitive effect" of rising prices on demand: more buyers thronged the market. The blank needs a verb meaning "to create / generate" demand. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — monetizing: making money from; the passage isn't about generating revenue from demand. ✗
  • Choice B — appraising: estimating value; the buyers aren't evaluating demand, they're creating it. ✗
  • Choice C — engendering: causing or producing; rising prices CAUSED more demand — exactly the counterintuitive effect. ✓
  • Choice D — exploiting: taking advantage of an existing thing; demand was being CREATED, not exploited. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
